fields.description:
# Overlay triggering: deterministic-where-low-FP, LLM-router otherwise

## Why
Running every overlay on every plan is wasteful and noisy; the router decides relevance. Some overlays have a reliable deterministic trigger (keyword/signal) and should fire that way to avoid an LLM call; the rest need a judgment pass.

## What
A two-path overlay trigger: a deterministic detector where a low-FP signal exists, with an LLM relevance pass as the fallback/for ambiguous cases.

## Scope
- Overlays (the full v4 §5 set — do not subset): T1 prior-art/novel-arch, T2 empirical-probe, T3 integration-feasibility, T4 compat/destructiveness, T5a perf, T5b reliability, T5c security, T5d accessibility, T5e maintainability, T6 UX, T7 docs, T8 LLM-antipatterns, T9 shared-state. (T2/T3/T4/T5d were dropped from an earlier experiment subset and restored — see criteria-registry-reconciliation.md; T3/T4/T5d still lack coverage data.), T10 infrastructure/IaC, T11 data-migration/backfill safety, T12 rollout/rollback/reversibility (T10 deterministic-triggered on IaC keywords; T11/T12 on data-change / deployed-system signals).
- Deterministic triggers (low-FP, VALIDATED round 4 — agree 12-13/13 with the LLM router, ~0 false-positive): T1 prior-art <- the 6 bright-lines; T5a performance <- I/O/compute/LLM-call/batch signals; T5d accessibility <- UI-keyword count.
- LLM-router relevance pass for the overlays whose keyword trigger is HIGH-FP (validated round 4): T8 LLM-antipatterns (keyword-matches almost every LLM ticket — 9/13 fire but only 3 relevant), T6 UX, T5b reliability, T5c security, T9 shared-state; plus confirming ambiguous deterministic hits. T8 MUST be in the active set for LLM/agent-design plans — it recovers structural-gap signal (enum-vocabulary/protocol-co-location gaps) a generic checklist misses.
- Calibration: track each overlay's downstream dismissal rate (over/under-triggering) per the observability substrate.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A deterministic trigger per overlay that has a low-FP signal, unit-tested for precision on fixtures.
- [ ] An LLM relevance pass covering the remaining overlays + ambiguous deterministic hits.
- [ ] Each overlay descriptor declares whether it triggers deterministically or by LLM, in the registry.
- [ ] Overlay fire/dismissal is recorded in REVIEW_RESULT for offline calibration.
